lotka_volterra.name: lotka_volterra
lotka_volterra.insight: Lotka-Volterra: bilinear term x*y = product of two EML-1 variables. EML-2 per step. Conserved quantity V involves ln(x), ln(y): also EML-2 (log is depth-2 via: ln(x) = eml(0, x) + 1). Periodic orbits: not chaotic, EML-2 governs all dynamics.

michaelis_menten.name: michaelis_menten
michaelis_menten.insight: v = Vmax*S/(Km+S): numerator is EML-1 (linear in S). Denominator (Km+S) is EML-1. Quotient needs reciprocal: 1/x = exp(-ln(x)) = EML-2. Full rate function is EML-2. Michaelis-Menten saturation kinetics = EML-2.

sir_model.name: SIR_epidemic
sir_model.insight: SIR model with R0=3.00: force of infection beta*S*I is bilinear → EML-2. Same structure as Lotka-Volterra (predator-prey). Epidemic dynamics and ecological oscillations are EML-2 systems.

fitzhugh_nagumo.name: fitzhugh_nagumo
fitzhugh_nagumo.insight: FitzHugh-Nagumo: dv/dt = v - v³/3 - w + I. The cubic term v³ = v*v*v needs 2 multiplies: depth 2 in isolation. Inside the full RHS expression (sum with w and I), the tree depth is 3. This is the minimal neuron model with cubic spiking mechanism = EML-3.

brusselator.name: brusselator
brusselator.insight: Brusselator (A=1.0, B=3.0): autocatalytic x²*y term. x²*y = x*(x*y) needs two multiplies → depth 3. Oscillating (B > 1+A²). Chemical oscillators require at least EML-3 (trimolecular autocatalysis).

exponential_growth.verdict: EML-1: linear ODE, exact exp solution

sir_model.verdict: EML-2: bilinear S*I force of infection

hill_function_n2.verdict: EML-4: x^n = exp(n*ln(x)) at depth 3, then rational at depth 4

turing_reaction_diffusion.verdict: EML-3 RHS (if Brusselator kinetics) + EML-2 Laplacian (finite diff)

threshold_switch.verdict: EML-inf: step function is not real-analytic

key_insight: Biology is EML-finite. Linear biology=EML-1, mass-action kinetics=EML-2, cooperative/allosteric=EML-3-4, threshold switches=EML-inf. The jump from EML-2 to EML-3 corresponds to trimolecular reactions and cubic nonlinearities — the minimum needed for chemical oscillations. Same EML hierarchy as physics: harmonic (EML-2) < anharmonic (EML-3) < chaotic.

findings.name: Mass-action kinetics = EML-2
findings.content: All bimolecular (A+B→C) reactions have EML-2 rate equations via bilinear terms.

findings.name: Chemical oscillations need EML-3
findings.content: Brusselator x²y and FitzHugh-Nagumo v³ — both require EML-3 for limit cycles.

findings.name: Hill function cooperativity = EML-4
findings.content: x^n for n>1 requires exp(n*ln(x)) = depth 3, then rational = depth 4.

findings.name: Threshold switches = EML-inf
findings.content: Boolean gene networks and step-function models are EML-inf (non-analytic).

findings.name: Biology follows universal EML hierarchy
findings.content: Linear → quadratic → cubic → EML-inf maps onto simple growth → ecosystems → neurons → digital logic. The same hierarchy as physics (harmonic → anharmonic → chaotic).

next_session.title: Financial Mathematics — EML Expansion
priorities: Heston stochastic volatility: EML-2 (sqrt(v) is EML-2 via exp(0.5*ln(v)))
Exotic options: barrier, Asian, lookback — EML depth analysis
Risk measures: VaR, CVaR — EML structure of quantile functions
